title: epipactis helleborine subsp. lusitanica
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Iberian helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Epipactis helleborine subsp. lusitanica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
